content.full_text:
Rc:
Farn Por-rrrcAt- Pnncrrcns CoMMrssroN
-128 J Street r Suite 620 r Sacramento. CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 o Fax (916) 322-0886
Aprrl22,2008
Lindsay A. Thorson
Adkinson, Andelson, Loya, Ruud & Romo
17871Park Plaza Drive
Cerritos, C A 907 03 -8697
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. I-08-026a
Dear Ms. Thorson
This letter supplements ollr letter of April 8, 2008 (Tfursrn Atlvice Letter, No.
I-08-026), and is based on additional facts you have provided antl a clarif ication as to
yollr reqltest for advice. You indicate that you are not requesting advice as to past
conduct, but rather, you are inquiring as to whether the Sierra Stnds Unificd School
District may enter into a contract with Douglas E. Barnhart, Inc. without r, irtlating the
Political Reform Act.l Per our telephone conrerration, you have also askcd our written
advice as to whether Paul Westberg is a public official, given the additrt-rnal facts you
have provided.
QUESTTONS
1. May the Sierra Sands Unified School District enter into a building contract
with Douglas E. Barnhart, Inc. after Paul Westberg, an employee of Westberg+White,
provided architectural services to the District under a contract between Westberg+White
and the District while Douglas E. Barnhart, Inc. was a tenant in a building owned by a
company in which Mr. Westberg held a 25 percent ownership interest?
2. Is Mr. Westberg a "public official" under Section 82048?
CONCLUSIONS
' The Political Reform Ast is contained in Government Code Sestions ttl(XX) through 9101.1. All
statutory ret'erences are to the Governnrent Code, unless rrtherwise indicated. 1'he rcgulations of the Fair
Political Practices Comnrission are contained in Sections ltl l l0 through lti997 olTitle 2 of the Calitornia
Code of Regulations. All regulatory ret'erences are to Title 2. Division 6 of the Calilirrnia Code of
Regulations. unless otherwise indicated.


File No. I-08-026
Page No. 2
1. The Act does not prohibit the District from awarding a building contract to
Douglas E. Bamhart, Inc. based on any actllal or perceived economic interest Mr.
Westberg may have had in Barnhart.
2. Mr. Westberg is not a "consultant" of the District, and therefore, not a "public
official" under the Act.
FACTS
In May 2006 the Sierra Sands Unified School District (the "District") entered into
a contract for architectural services with Westberg+White under which Westberg+White
would, among other things, prepare plans and specifications for various District
construction and modernization projects (the "Projects"). The architectural services are
to be provided over a period of several years, concluding with the completion of the
Projects. Several Westberg+White architects, including Paul Westberg who is a principal
of the firm, perform work under this contract.
In November 2007, the District issued a Request for Qualification ("RFQ") and
thereafter conducted interviews of some of the builders who responded to the RFQ.
Douglas E. Bamhart, Inc. ("Barnhart") was one of three builders who were interviewed.
Mr. Westberg was present during discussions regarding the submitted proposals and
offered his opinion, but had no vote in the selection of the interviewees. He, along with
the District Superintendent, the District Assistant Superintendent, the Principal of Faller
Elementary School and the Principal of Burroughs High School (collectively the "District
Staff'), and the District legal counsel, were present during the interviews. After the
interviews, the District Staff unanimously selected Barnhart for recommendation to the
Board as the builder for the Projects.
Subsequently, it was brought to the District's attention that Bamhart was a tenant
in a building owned by SLO Newport, Inc., a corporation in which Mr. Westberg is a 25
percent shareholder. On February 8, 2008, SLO Newport, Inc. sold that building to a
third party. Neither SLO Newport, Inc. nor Mr. Westberg retains any ownership interest
in the building. The District is now contemplating entering into a contract with Barnhart
for construction services for the Projects.
The work performed by Mr. Westberg under the Westberg+White contract
consists of the following: reviewing the design and construction documents that were
prepared by other architects at Westberg+White, attending and answering questions at
planning meetings at the school sites, coordinating the submission of plans and
specifications for the Projects to the Division of the State Architect, Office of Public
School Construction and the Califomia Department of Education, attending the selection
meetings regarding proposals submitted by builders and attending interviews of builder
candidates. Mr. Westberg voiced his opinions on possible builder candidates in meetings
with District staff. The purpose of Mr. Westberg's opinions was to inform the District in


File No. I-08-026
Page No. 3
its decision. He did not participate in District staff's selection of candidates to
recommend to the Board of Education, the District's decisionmaker. District Staff
independently reviewed and evaluated all proposals, references, interview answers and
other materials provided by builder candidates. Ultimately, District staff made their own
recommendations to the Board of Education.
It is contemplated that Mr. Westberg will continLle to meet with representatives of
the District from time to time on an "as needed" basis. He is not the main architect on
these Projects but, as a principal of Westberg+White, does talk with the main architect as
questions may arise. He does not participate in any regularly scheduled meetings
regarding the Projects.
Mr. Westberg does not meet any of the factors that would qualify him as a
consultant under Regulation 18701(a)(2)(A). Furtheffnore, Mr. Westberg's position is
not designated in the District's conflict-of-interest code and he does not perform duties
that a District staff member would perform. The District does not employ any architects
on its staff.
ANALYSN
Ouestion I
As stated in our previous letter, when a public official participates in a
governmental decision in violation of the Act's conflict-of-interest provisions, the Act
does not automatically invalidate that decision. Instead, if the decision is challenged in
litigation, the court may, in its discretion, invalidate the decision. (See Section
91003(b).) If, however, Mr. Westberg is not a public official, then he is not subject to the
conflict-of-interest provisions of the Act so that Section 91003(b) would not be
applicable.
Ouestion 2
Under Regulation 18701(a)(2), there are two ways that an individual can become
a "consultant" and, therefore, a "public official" as defined in Section 82048.
First, an individual may be a consultant if he or she performs, pursuant to a
contract, any of the actions described in Regulation 18701(aX2)(A). Your facts indicate
that Mr. Westberg does not perform any of those actions under the contract between the
District and Westberg+White.
Second, an individual may be a consultant if he or she "serves in a staff capacity
with the agency" under Regulation 18701(aX2)(B). This phrase has been construed by
the Commission to include only those individuals who are performing substantially all
the same tasks that normally would be performed by one or more staff members of a
governmental agency. (Conley Advice Letter, No. 4-96-182; Randolplz Advice Letter,


File No. I-08-026
Page No.4
No. I-95-045.) Persons who work on one project or a limited number of projects for an
agency over a limited period of time are not deemed to be serving in a staff capacity.
(sonchez Advice Letter, No. 4-97-438; Thomas Advice Letter, No. 4-96-053; parm
Advice Letter, No. I-95-064 Randolplz Advice Letter, supra.)
The work Mr. Westberg performs for the District under the contract between the
District and Westberg+White pertains to a limited number of projects over a limited
period of time. Once the Projects are completed, the relationship between Mr. Westberg
and the District will terminate. No ongoing relationship is contemplated. While the
Projects themselves will span a period of years, the work performed by Mr. Westberg is
sporadic, with most of the contract work performed by other architects of
Westberg+White. His work does not give rise to the kind of ongoing relationship
contemplated by the phrase "serves in a staff capacity." Rather, it resembles the kind of
periodic or sporadic work described in the Sanchez Advice Letter (supra), and the Maze
Advice Letter (No. I-95-296), where we advised that the individual did not serve in a
staff capacity. In Sanchez., a contractor performed periodic biological and physical
surveys on a project over a two-year period. In Maze, employees of an accounting firm
performed annual audits of municipal govemment entities pursuant to a multi-year
contract.
Based on the facts you have provided, we conclude that Mr. Westberg does not
serve the District in a staff capacity under Regulation 1 870 1(a)(2) and is not a consulrant
or public official under Section 82048.2 Accordingly, he is not subject to the Act's
confl ict-of-interest provisions.
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
General Counsel
By: Valentina Joyce
Counsel, Legal Division
VJ:jgl
2 However, we caution that. if in the future, Mr. Westberg will be performing additional work for
the District. that work, together with the work described in your letters, must be re-evaluated to determine
ifthe facts have changed so that his overall activities are no longer "sporadic" but "regular and continuous"
so as to reflect an "ongoing relationship." (See f/arris Advice Letter, No. 4-02-239., Stnith Advice Letter.
No. I-99-3 16.) If that occurs, Mr. Westberg will be required to file Statements of Eeonomic Interests and
will be subject to the Act's contlict-ot'-interest provisions.

regarding the Projects.
Mr. Westberg does not meet any of the factors that would qualify him as a
consultant under Regulation 18701(a)(2)(A). Furtheffnore, Mr. Westberg's position is
not designated in the District's conflict-of-interest code and he does not perform duties
that a District staff member would perform. The District does not employ any architects
on its staff.
